steps.title: Use the zeros on the graph
steps.content: Values of $x$ where $f(x)=0$ are the $x$-intercepts of the graph. From the graph, the curve meets the $x$-axis at two distinct places.

steps.title: Count the distinct values
steps.content: Because there are two distinct $x$-intercepts, there are two distinct values of $x$ for which $f(x)=0$. The answer is B.
